title: Video coding using frame rotation
abstract: Video frames are rotated for encoding and decoding blocks therein. During an encoding process, a number of degrees to rotate a video frame is determined. The video frame is rotated to produce rotated pixel values. A prediction residual is then generated for a block to be encoded based on the rotated pixel values. That block is encoded based on the prediction residual. During a decoding process, an encoded block is decoded to produce inverse transform pixel values, and a number of degrees to inverse rotate an encoded video frame is determined. The encoded video frame is inverse rotated to produce inverse rotated pixel values corresponding to the inverse transform pixel values. The inverse rotated pixel values are then outputted to an output video stream. One or more syntax elements indicating the number of degrees can be encoded to a bitstream during encoding and decoded from the bitstream during decoding.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. A method for encoding a current block of a video frame, the method comprising: determining a number of degrees by which to rotate at least a portion of the video frame; rotating the at least a portion of the video frame according to the number of degrees to produce rotated pixel values for the at least a portion of the video frame; generating a prediction residual for the current block based on the rotated pixel values; and encoding the current block based on the prediction residual. 2. The method of claim 1, further comprising: encoding one or more syntax elements indicative of the number of degrees to a bitstream to which the current block is encoded. 3. The method of claim 1 or 2, wherein determining the number of degrees by which to rotate the at least a portion of the video frame comprises: determining rate-distortion values resulting from rotating the at least a portion of the video frame according to respective numbers of degrees; and identifying one of the respective numbers of degrees resulting in a lowest one of the rate-distortion values. 4. The method of any of claims 1 to 3, wherein rotating the at least a portion of the video frame according to the number of degrees to produce the rotated pixel values for the at least a portion of the video frame comprises: performing a linear transformation against pixel values of the at least a portion of the video frame according to the number of degrees. 5. The method of any of claims 1 to 4, wherein rotating the at least a portion of the video frame according to the number of degrees changes a scan order for encoding blocks of the video frame. 6. The method of any of claims 1 to 5, wherein the number of degrees is one of 0 

 degrees, 90 degrees, 180 degrees, or 270 degrees. 7. A method for decoding an encoded block of an encoded video frame, the method comprising: decoding the encoded block to produce inverse transform pixel values; determining a number of degrees by which to inverse rotate at least a portion of the encoded video frame; inverse rotating the at least a portion of the encoded video frame according to the number of degrees to produce inverse rotated pixel values for the inverse transform pixel values corresponding to the at least a portion of the encoded video frame; and outputting the inverse rotated pixel values to an output video stream. 8. The method of claim 7, wherein determining the number of degrees by which to inverse rotate the at least a portion of the encoded video frame comprises: decoding one or more syntax elements indicative of the number of degrees from an encoded bitstream including the encoded video frame. 9. The method of claim 8, wherein the one or more syntax elements includes two bits, wherein respective values of the two bits correspond to different numbers of degrees available for decoding the encoded block, wherein the number of degrees is one of the different numbers of degrees. 10. The method of any of claims 7 to 9, wherein inverse rotating the at least a portion of the encoded video frame according to the number of degrees to produce inverse rotated pixel values for the inverse transform pixel values corresponding to the at least a portion of the encoded video frame comprises: performing a linear transformation against at least some of the inverse transform pixel values according to the number of degrees, the at least some of the inverse transform pixel values located in the at least a portion of the encoded video frame. 11. The method of any of claims 7 to 10, wherein the at least a portion of the encoded video frame is inverse rotated according to the number of degrees subsequent to reconstructing the encoded video frame. 12. The method of any of claims 7 to 11, wherein a scan order for decoding encoded blocks of the encoded video frame is based on a rotation of the encoded video frame during encoding. 13. The method of any of claims 7 to 12, wherein the number of degrees is one of 0 degrees, 90 degrees, 180 degrees, or 270 degrees. 14. An apparatus for decoding an encoded block of an encoded video frame, the apparatus comprising: a processor configured to execute instructions stored in a non-transitory storage medium to: decode the encoded block to produce inverse transform pixel values; determine a number of degrees by which to inverse rotate at least a portion of the encoded video frame; inverse rotate the at least a portion of the encoded video frame according to the number of degrees to produce inverse rotated pixel values for the inverse transform pixel values that correspond to the at least a portion of the encoded video frame; and output the inverse rotated pixel values to an output video stream. 15. The apparatus of claim 14, wherein the instructions to determine the number of degrees by which to inverse rotate the at least a portion of the encoded video frame include instructions to: decode one or more syntax elements indicative of the number of degrees from an encoded bitstream including the encoded video frame. 16. The apparatus of claim 15, wherein the one or more syntax elements includes two bits, wherein respective values of the two bits correspond to different numbers of degrees available for decoding the encoded block, wherein the number of degrees is one of the different numbers of degrees. 17. The apparatus of any of claims 14 to 16, wherein the instructions to inverse rotate the at least a portion of the encoded video frame according to the number of degrees to produce inverse rotated pixel values for the inverse transform coefficients that correspond to the at least a portion of the encoded video frame include instructions to: 

 perform a linear transformation against at least some of the inverse transform pixel values according to the number of degrees, the at least some of the inverse transform pixel values located in the at least a portion of the encoded video frame. 18. The apparatus of any of claims 14 to 17, wherein at least the portion of the encoded video frame is inverse rotated according to the number of degrees subsequent to a reconstruction of the encoded video frame. 19. The apparatus of any claims 14 to 18, wherein a raster order for decoding encoded blocks of the encoded video frame is based on a rotation of the encoded video frame during encoding. 20. The apparatus of any of claims 14 to 19, wherein the number of degrees is one of 0 degrees, 90 degrees, 180 degrees, or 270 degrees. 21. An apparatus for encoding a current block of a video frame, the apparatus comprising: a processor configured to execute instructions stored in a non-transitory storage medium to: determine a number of degrees by which to rotate at least a portion of the video frame; rotate the at least a portion of the video frame according to the number of degrees to produce rotated pixel values for the at least a portion of the video frame; generate a prediction residual for the current block based on the rotated pixel values; and encode the current block based on the prediction residual.
